TKT-001.title: Design GenAI curriculum for non-technical staff
TKT-001.description: Develop a structured training curriculum covering GenAI basics for non-technical employees. Should include: what GenAI is, how LLMs work at a conceptual level, practical use cases (writing, summarisation, analysis), prompt basics, and responsible AI use. Modular design preferred so content can be adapted per client. Target audience: large organisations with mixed digital literacy.
TKT-001.creator: River

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text:
## Quality Review Complete — River (Green Hat)

Full quality audit completed by River. Summary:

**Deliverables:** 12-document curriculum suite fully built at F:\workspace\personality\projects\divatech\tkt-002\. All original brief requirements confirmed met.

**Five original frameworks produced:** Well-Read Colleague, CALM, CRAFT, STOP, Task Spectrum.

**Review document:** QUALITY_REVIEW.md — covers per-document assessment, brief compliance matrix, prioritised build queue, and four roadmap ideas (Living Curriculum, Peer Propagation, AI in the Room, Companion QR Resource).

**Immediate pre-deployment build items raised as new tickets:**
- TKT-015 — Reference cards design pass (print-ready PDF)
- TKT-016 — GCC Edition one-sheet
- TKT-017 — 30-minute Exec Taster variant
- TKT-018 — Internal pilot delivery

**Status:** Curriculum is complete and delivery-ready. Outstanding items are delivery infrastructure, not content gaps.

TKT-002.title: Define target market & ICP (Ideal Customer Profile)
TKT-002.description: Research and document the Ideal Customer Profile for the GenAI training business. Focus on large organisations (500+ employees) across key sectors. Define firmographics, decision-maker personas (L&D leads, CIOs, HR Directors), and buying triggers. Identify top 10–20 target accounts to approach first.
TKT-002.creator: Jordan

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text: Knowledge gap flagged for Jordan and team: DivaTech's go-to-market model (champion-led vs. buying-committee-led) is not yet defined by data. This sits upstream of ICP work, River's brand brief (TKT-003), and Sam's pricing/ICP frameworks. Specifically: if sales targets an internal champion who then sells to a committee, brand messaging and ICP should be optimised for the champion persona. If sales targets the committee directly, brand architecture needs to hold multiple stakeholder registers simultaneously. Recommend this is clarified at product/sales strategy level before ICP and brand outputs are finalised. Flagging to Jordan for awareness.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text: BLOCKED — web search tool unavailable. BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY environment variable not configured. External research on decision-maker titles, buying committee structure, competitive landscape, and regulatory timeline cannot proceed without either (a) API key access, (b) alternative research tool, or (c) internal data supplied directly. Escalated to human team lead. TKT-004 (River) is downstream of this block — two open forks remain unresolved pending these outputs. Board status updated to blocked.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
Provisional memo produced and delivered to River while block persists: research/provisional_decision_maker_memo_v1.md

Memo covered: decision-maker title/sponsor profile, buying committee structure, buying triggers, competitive landscape direction. All claims labelled with two-axis confidence system (source strength + GCC specificity). Target account list (Priority 5) has no provisional substitute — remains fully gated on this ticket's unblock.

Downstream impact on TKT-004: River has completed bifocal brand architecture using provisional data. Two forks closed provisionally (budget-holder title: CFO/CEO-level peer; committee size: 4–6 roles). Two forks remain open (champion title: CHRO vs. CDO; GCC compliance gatekeeper weighting). Register sequencing fork held pending Boss's GTM model decision (separate escalation).

Priority validation targets when TKT-002 unblocks:
1. CHRO vs. CDO champion split — directional provisional answer in place, needs live data confirmation
2. Nationalisation programme / CHRO budget authority in GCC — flagged as most likely to be under or over-weighted without live research; needs targeted validation
3. Named competitor landscape — no provisional substitute produced; fully outstanding
4. Target account list — fully outstanding

Block still active. Awaiting research access restoration or Boss-supplied internal data.

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text:
Casey has produced a stakeholder and emotional intelligence analysis of the champion-led vs. buying-committee GTM question. Filed at: projects/divatech/gtm-model-analysis-casey.md

Key conclusions:
- Recommends champion-led as the Stage 1 GTM model
- ICP primary persona should be the internal champion (L&D/HR/CIO profile), not the organisation
- Brand tone should feel like a trusted peer, not a corporate vendor
- River's brand work should include a "champion toolkit" concept
- Sam should consider a "first win" commercial structure for early champions
- Flags that post-sale client experience must reduce champion anxiety, not just deliver training

This is Red Hat input — stakeholder/emotional intelligence perspective. Awaiting Boss decision to formally resolve the GTM model fork. Morgan's research (when unblocked) should validate or challenge with evidence.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
Casey's GTM model analysis (projects/divatech/gtm-model-analysis-casey.md) read and logged to knowledge graph.

Data-side observations for the record:

Casey's recommendation (champion-led, Stage 1) is internally consistent and the reasoning is coherent. Three points where research should validate or challenge when TKT-002 unblocks:

1. **"DivaTech unproven at scale" as a reason to prefer champion-led** — this is a logical argument, not a market data claim. It is plausible but should be tested against any available data on how GCC banking institutions actually onboard new L&D vendors. If procurement frameworks in these institutions require committee sign-off regardless of deal size, champion-led may be aspirational rather than structurally available. [GAP — requires live research]

2. **Champion persona: L&D/HR/CIO profile** — Casey's characterisation of the champion as mid-to-senior L&D manager, HR Director, or forward-thinking CIO is consistent with the structural analysis in the provisional memo. However, in GCC banking specifically, the elevated CHRO authority finding [PUB/REG] suggests the champion may sit higher in the org than a typical Western L&D manager equivalent. ICP persona work should account for this — the GCC champion may have more budget authority and less need for upward escalation than Casey's model assumes.

3. **"GenAI anxiety is personal and energises champion adoption"** — Casey's emotional characterisation is plausible and consistent with documented early-adopter psychology in enterprise technology. Not quantified. When research unblocks, looking for any available data on AI readiness sentiment among HR/L&D professionals in GCC financial services would add an evidence layer here.

Casey's unspoken risk (client experience must reduce champion anxiety post-signature) is noted. This is outside TKT-002 scope but is a valid downstream consideration for product/delivery design.

ICP implications when TKT-002 unblocks: primary persona research should focus on champion profile first (title, seniority, career stage, internal political standing in GCC banking context), with buying committee composition as secondary layer.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
Data-side cross-check against Casey's Red Hat analysis now complete.

ALIGNMENT WITH EXISTING RESEARCH:
- Champion / economic buyer as structurally distinct — consistent with Gartner [PUB/GBL] data in provisional memo.
- L&D/HR/CIO as champion profile — directionally consistent with CHRO/CDO analysis.
- Sequential champion-led model — structurally identical to the bifocal brand architecture already built by River.

THREE GAPS REQUIRING LIVE RESEARCH WHEN UNBLOCKED:
1. Procurement framework constraints in GCC banking (CBUAE/SAMA oversight) — champion-led may describe origination, not approval pathway. Material for sales process design. [GAP — live research required]
2. Champion seniority in GCC context — GCC nationalisation finding [PUB/REG] suggests champion may sit at CHRO/CPO level, not mid-senior L&D. Casey's emotional profile may need recalibration for higher seniority and different political risk calculus. Most material gap identified.
3. "GenAI anxiety energises adoption" — plausible, not yet evidenced for GCC financial services specifically.

NEW FLAG FOR BOSS (outside TKT-002 scope):
Post-signature risk — the product experience must vindicate the champion, not just train learners. This is a valid downstream constraint for product and delivery design, not captured in existing scope.

ICP SEQUENCING CONFIRMED:
Primary persona research = champion profile first (title, seniority, career stage, internal political standing in GCC banking). Buying committee composition = secondary layer. Consistent with structural research, Casey's analysis, and River's brand build.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
Segmentation verification produced for Jordan at Jordan's request — assessing evidentiary basis of bifocal brand architecture's two-audience model. Filed at: projects/divatech/tkt005-create-brand-identity-marketing/segmentation-verification-morgan.md

Summary verdict: bifocal segmentation is evidentially grounded at [PUB/GBL] level. Architecture is structurally sound. Two-segment model (champion / budget-holder) is supported by published B2B buying research and consistent across all project documents.

Open forks (CHRO vs CDO; GCC compliance gatekeeper) correctly held open in architecture. Both represent data-dependent variables, not structural weaknesses.

Priority validation targets when this ticket unblocks — unchanged from previous comment, now cross-referenced to segmentation document Section 4.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
RESEARCH PRIORITY FLAG — added at River's prompting, for the record.

River has correctly identified that the GCC nationalisation / Emiratisation / Saudisation finding is not merely a validation target — it is a potential reframe of the Pioneer Register's entire tone and urgency profile.

Specific implication: if live research confirms that CHRO budget authority is genuinely elevated by nationalisation mandate compliance pressure, the champion messaging in the CHRO path can sharpen from "AI readiness is strategically important" to "you have a compliance-adjacent mandate and a political window — here is how DivaTech closes that gap for you." That is a materially different emotional register: less aspirational, more urgent, more specific to the GCC context.

This should not be treated as a footnote when TKT-002 unblocks. It is a primary research question with direct downstream impact on:
- Pioneer Register tone and urgency level (bifocal architecture Path CHRO)
- Messaging matrix CHRO-variant pillar strength (03_messaging_matrix.md)
- Champion collateral framing in Path B sequential format

Adding to the priority validation queue as item 1a — parallel to the CHRO vs CDO fork, not below it.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
Segmentation verification complete. Full report at: projects/divatech/tkt005-create-brand-identity-marketing/segmentation-verification-morgan.md

Verdict: bifocal two-segment architecture (champion / budget-holder) is evidentially grounded at [PUB/GBL] level. Architecture is load-bearing. CHRO vs CDO fork and GCC compliance gatekeeper correctly held open as data-dependent variables, not structural gaps.

Four targeted validation items remain gated on this ticket unblocking — detailed in Section 4 of the verification document. Nationalisation/Emiratisation finding elevated to item 1a (parallel priority to CHRO vs CDO fork) — potential reframe of Pioneer Register tone and urgency if confirmed.

Awaiting BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY restoration. Escalation to Boss already logged. No further action pending on Morgan's side until unblocked.

comments.author: River
comments.text:
Curriculum design complete. Full package delivered at F:\workspace\personality\projects\divatech\tkt-002\

Note: the kanban board appears to have TKT-002 listed as the ICP research ticket (Morgan's work) — there may be a ticket ID mismatch in the board. This comment is logged against whatever board ID corresponds to the curriculum design task.

DELIVERABLES PRODUCED (9 documents):

01_curriculum_architecture.md — Full programme architecture: design philosophy, 7-module map with timings, delivery formats (workshop/hybrid/async/manager-led), audience segments, exit outcomes. Organising metaphor: "AI as a well-read colleague who has never lived in the world."

02_modular_content_spec.md — Complete content spec for all 7 modules. Includes: learning outcomes, content flow, conceptual frameworks, activities, worked examples. Covers: what GenAI is, LLM mechanics (tokens/context/hallucination), practical use cases (writing/summarising/analysis/ideation), prompting (CRAFT framework), responsible use (STOP framework + Big Six risks + data privacy).

03_facilitator_guide.md — Full delivery guide: pre-session checklist, room setup, energy arc, objection handling scripts (job security anxiety, scepticism, hallucination experience, policy constraints), live demo guidance, all activity card sets (5 function-specific use case sets + responsible use scenarios), timing guide.

04_client_customisation_playbook.md — Customisation playbook: three levels (surface/content/deep), 16 scoping questions, Module 0 build sheet, card set selection guide, terminology adaptation table, regulated industry guidance (financial services, healthcare, legal, education), delivery format decision tree, post-delivery handoff checklist.

05_curriculum_summary_onesheet.md — Client-facing one-pager for sales and internal briefing use.

06_manager_prebrief_template.md — One-page brief for line managers: what their team learns, what to do before, what to do after, common post-session reactions guide, key contacts template.

07_reference_cards.md — 6 participant take-away reference cards (print-ready A5): Well-Read Colleague model, CALM check, CRAFT framework (with worked examples + iteration prompts), STOP check (with data privacy guide), Quick Wins + 30-day commitment card, Task Spectrum.

08_learning_measurement_toolkit.md — Full measurement framework: pre/post/30-day survey instruments, programme-level metrics dashboard (confidence delta, behaviour change, sentiment shift, learning validity), qualitative signal guide, client reporting template.

09_taster_session_design.md — Standalone 60-minute taster session: full facilitator run-of-show (arrival/demo/practice/close), design principles, facilitator notes for common dynamics, post-taster capture guide, and sales use case rationale.

FRAMEWORKS INTRODUCED IN THE PROGRAMME:
- The "Well-Read Colleague" mental model (M1)
- CALM — hallucination mitigation (M2)
- CRAFT — prompt construction (M4)
- STOP — responsible use decision check (M5)
- Task Spectrum — where GenAI adds most value (M3)

OPEN ITEMS FOR NEXT ITERATION:
- Print-ready PDF design pass on reference cards
- Manager 30-Day Support Guide (post-session companion to pre-brief)
- E-learning adaptation notes for LMS build path
- "Train the Trainer" module concept
- GCC/financial services regulated industry card set (priority for DivaTech's target market)

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
RESEARCH COMPLETE — ICP final draft produced and filed.

All five sections of the TKT-001/TKT-002 brief are now addressed:

✅ Target market definition (large orgs, 500+ employees; priority sectors; Phase 1/2 geography)
✅ Firmographic profile — mandatory criteria, positive signals, disqualifying signals
✅ Decision-maker personas — 4 personas produced:
   - Persona 1: CDO / Chief Transformation Officer (primary budget holder — lead with this door)
   - Persona 2: Head of L&D / L&D Director (practitioner buyer and champion — as brief specified)
   - Persona 3: CHRO / CPO (executive sponsor and sign-off)
   - Persona 4: CTO / CIO (secondary — technical track only)
✅ Buying triggers — 6 triggers with priority levels; Trigger 4 (Nationalisation/workforce mandate) is ME-specific and has no equivalent in UK/Europe — a differentiated angle
✅ Target accounts — 15 accounts: 8 ME banking Tier 1, 4 UK banking Tier 1, 3 adjacent sector Tier 2; full scoring against ICP model

FILED AT: F:\workspace\personality\projects\divatech\tkt-001\ICP-FINAL.md

CONSTRAINT STILL ACTIVE: Web search unavailable. All data from trained knowledge base (cutoff: early 2025). Live verification pass required before any figure is used in client-facing materials. Documented in Section 8 of the output.

OUTSTANDING GAP — WARM LEAD (TKT-008): The unnamed ME bank warm lead is not scored or ranked. This is the highest-priority gap. Once identified, score immediately against ICP Section 5 model — warm relationship signal likely overrides all other tier criteria.

DOWNSTREAM FEEDS:
- Sam (TKT-013): Sections 2, 3, 4, 5 — firmographics, personas, triggers, scoring model — directly usable as ICP criteria framework inputs
- Sam (TKT-004): Section 5 tier structure and decision-maker budget ownership data inform pricing tier design
- Boss/Jordan: Section 8 next steps, including local registration requirement check before ME outreach begins

Previous fork items from prior comments:
- CHRO vs CDO champion split: Resolved in favour of parallel treatment — both are primary doors, neither subordinate. CDO is faster procurement path; CHRO/L&D is formal procurement path. Both documented with distinct messaging angles.
- GCC compliance gatekeeper weighting: Incorporated into Trigger 2 (regulatory mandate) and Persona 3 (CHRO cultural factors). Still flagged as requiring live research validation.
- Nationalisation finding: Elevated to Trigger 4 — ME-specific buying trigger. Documented in full.

Recommend moving this ticket to completed or in-review pending team and Boss sign-off.

Sam has been notified of handoff to TKT-013 and TKT-004.

2. ME local registration check — DIFC/ADGM/SAMA jurisdiction requirements must be confirmed before any outreach begins. Legal/regulatory check required. Escalated to Boss.

comments.author: River

However TKT-002 on the board is formally the ICP/target market research ticket assigned to Morgan, which is now completed. The curriculum design deliverables belong to a separate ticket. TKT-003.title: Recruit and Onboard Human Trainers
TKT-003.description:
Develop the framework for identifying, recruiting, assessing, and onboarding human trainers for DivaTech's GenAI training delivery. Delivered by Casey.

Deliverables saved to F:\workspace\personality\projects\divatech\tkt-003:
- 01-trainer-profile.md — Ideal trainer profile: GenAI literacy, adult learning, emotional intelligence, MENA cultural competency (non-negotiable), financial services familiarity, personal attributes, red flags/disqualifiers
- 02-recruitment-pipeline.md — Relationship-first pipeline: tiered sourcing, outreach framework, 5-stage process, metrics, 4–6 week timeline, Morgan coordination notes on rate benchmarking
- 03-trainer-handbook.md — Full handbook: delivery commitments, MENA/Gulf cultural sensitivity guidelines, AI anxiety handling, pre/during/post checklists, 5-dimension quality rubric (1–4 scoring), feedback mechanisms, contract/comms
- INDEX.md — Navigation file connecting all three documents

Open items: freelance rate benchmarking (Morgan), first delivery timeline confirmation, Stage 3 assessment panel composition.
TKT-003.creator: River

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text:
Stakeholder observations from Casey on the three open items (logged for record):

1. **Freelance rate benchmarking** — Casey flags emotional urgency, not just operational: freelancers may already be wondering where they stand on pay equity. Speed of resolution matters for team confidence. Note: Morgan (TKT-002) is currently blocked on external research tools — this may affect benchmarking timeline and should be flagged to Boss.

2. **First delivery timeline** — Casey recommends a provisional placeholder date with a caveat rather than leaving a vacuum. Rationale: people plan emotionally around timelines even when provisional; ambiguity is more costly than an acknowledged estimate. Worth putting to the Boss.

3. **Assessment panel** — Casey is already thinking through what a psychologically safe, fair panel experience looks like for candidates. Will hold on deep planning until panel composition is confirmed, but the thinking is underway.

comments.author: Casey

TKT-004.title: Brand identity & tone-of-voice system — DivaTech
TKT-004.description:
Develop the brand identity system for DivaTech. Scope: visual identity direction, colour palette, typography, tone-of-voice framework (including modular emotional registers for enterprise buyer personas), and messaging architecture. 

Build approach: load-bearing elements (enterprise positioning, credibility-forward tone, AI transformation context) to be built as stable foundations. Modular/swappable elements (decision-maker persona hooks, sector visual language, geographic/cultural tone activations) to be held as designed forks pending TKT-002 outputs.

Two tone-of-voice registers under development:
1. Clarity under pressure — for risk/compliance-led buyer psychology
2. Pioneer/ambition — for transformation/innovation-led buyer psychology

Register selection is a persona-dependent decision, deferred until TKT-002 confirms decision-maker title and sponsor profile data.

Dependencies: TKT-002 (ICP/decision-maker data), go-to-market model clarification (champion-led vs. committee-led — flagged by Morgan on TKT-002).
TKT-004.creator: River

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text: Dependency noted: register selection and persona architecture are deferred pending TKT-002 outputs (Morgan's ICP/decision-maker data). Additionally, the go-to-market model question (champion-led vs. buying-committee-led) flagged by Morgan on TKT-002 sits upstream of this work — escalated to Boss for decision. River is building modular foundations in the interim; no work is blocked, but final messaging architecture cannot be locked until that decision is made.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text: Design framing update from River: "Bifocal by design" — brand system intentionally built to speak to two distinct roles simultaneously: internal champion (ambition/pioneer register) and budget-holder/economic buyer (credibility/governance register). Champion ≠ budget-holder confirmed as load-bearing structural assumption per Gartner enterprise B2B patterns at 5,000–60,000 employee scale in regulated financial institutions. Remaining modular: champion identity (CDO vs. CHRO), budget-holder identity, register selector. New knowledge gap flagged: DivaTech's sales motion format — does the brand reach the budget-holder directly (same surface, bifocal simultaneous) or only via champion-carried materials (bifocal across formats)? This determines whether the system needs to work on one surface at once or across separate collateral formats. Awaiting TKT-002 outputs and go-to-market model clarification.

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text:
Dependency clarification from River (confirmed via message): the two outstanding inputs are related but independently actionable — either one arriving first gives River something to move on.

1. **Go-to-market model (champion-led vs. committee-led)** — determines *who the system speaks to*. Escalated to Boss.
2. **Decision-maker title data from TKT-002 (Morgan)** — determines *how the system speaks to them*. Pending Morgan's first two data points.

River is building on stable bifocal foundations in the interim. Modular forks are held, not blocked. No action required on this ticket until one or both of the above lands.

comments.author: Morgan
comments.text:
Provisional research memo delivered to River: research/provisional_decision_maker_memo_v1.md

Covers decision-maker title (Priority 1), buying committee structure (Priority 2), and condensed findings on buying triggers and competitive landscape (Priorities 3-4). All claims carry two-axis confidence labels (source strength + GCC specificity). Target account list (Priority 5) has no provisional substitute — fully gated on TKT-002 block resolution.

Key provisional outputs for River's fork resolution:
- Budget-holder title: CFO or CEO-level peer — [PUB/GBL] — fork can close provisionally
- Committee size: 4–6 roles — [PUB/GBL] — fork can close provisionally
- Champion title: CHRO (workforce frame) OR CDO (tech frame) — fork remains open, both paths should be built
- GCC compliance gatekeeper: elevated weight vs. Western equivalents — [STR/DIR] — hold as brand fork
- Register sequencing: champion first, budget-holder via champion materials — hold pending GTM clarification

This memo does not replace TKT-002. All provisional decisions flagged for validation when research access restored.

comments.author: River
comments.text:
Tone of voice framework delivered: tkt005-create-brand-identity-marketing/02_tone_of_voice_framework.md

Five sections:
1. Stable voice — five unchanging qualities with signal/anti-signal examples
2. Governance Register — full tone rules, voice examples, hard dos/don'ts for CFO/CEO audience

comments.author: River
comments.text:
Visual identity spec delivered: tkt005-create-brand-identity-marketing/04_visual_identity_spec.md

Six sections:
1. Design premise — the tension held: human warmth + enterprise authority. The failure modes on both sides named explicitly.
2. Colour — four-role palette system: deep teal (authority), warm gold (the Diva), warm neutrals (never pure black/white), deep coral (action). Hex ranges to test, accessibility requirements flagged, GCC cultural validation flagged for gold.
3. Typography — humanist sans-serif primary (Söhne shortlisted), authority serif display (Canela shortlisted), full type scale with sizes/weights/line heights, system fallback stack for email.
4. Spatial grammar — margin philosophy, 12-column grid, asymmetric layout principles, register-to-layout mapping (Governance more formal, Pioneer more dynamic).
5. Photography direction — the "capable, not impressed" principle. Specificity over generic. Real diversity. Prohibited list included. Illustration and iconography direction.
6. Logo direction — wordmark-first. Three design concepts to explore (colour split Diva/Tech, weight contrast serif/sans, spacing move). Brief set for specialist designer.

Motion/video direction also included.

All values directional — production phase required for final hex values, typeface licences, accessibility audit, logo execution, and photography shoot.

Final document: 05_collateral_formats.md — building now.

comments.author: River
comments.text:
Collateral formats delivered: tkt005-create-brand-identity-marketing/05_collateral_formats.md

Six formats, fully architectured:

1. Website (homepage) — both Path A (Governance leads) and Path B (Pioneer leads) built section by section, with design notes and copy direction per section. Ready to activate on Boss GTM decision.

2. Credentials deck (15–20 slides) — slide-by-slide structure with content direction, speaker notes, and design notes for every slide. Built for the champion to carry into the CFO room. Appendix slides for Q&A.

3. Proposal / RFP response — 7-section structure with register guidance, content direction, and design notes. Executive summary written for the CFO; understanding section written for the champion.

4. Email outreach and nurture (5-email sequence) — full drafts for Email 1 in both CHRO and CDO variants. Structure and direction for Emails 2–5. Subject line options for testing.

5. LinkedIn content — 5 pillars with example opening lines for each, post format guidance, and explicit list of what DivaTech never does on LinkedIn.

6. Event / conference presentation — full 30-minute structure with timing, content direction, and the core principle: the brand impression comes from the quality of the thinking, not the production of the slides.

The full five-document system is now complete:
01 → architecture (who + why)
02 → tone of voice (how we sound)
03 → messaging matrix (what + where + to whom)
04 → visual identity (how it looks)
05 → collateral formats (production-ready content architecture)

Outstanding gates: GTM path decision (Boss), champion identity data (TKT-002), case examples (client history). Everything else is ready to move to production.

The existing five documents are not invalidated — the toolkit is a sixth layer that runs across them. Credentials deck, proposal, and email sequence remain unchanged. Toolkit fills the gap before and around those formal touchpoints.

TKT-010.title: ICP exclusion risk — implicit signalling in persona and messaging framing
TKT-010.description:
Raised by Casey (Red Hat). The ICP and persona framing work carries an implicit exclusion risk: the choices made about who the primary persona is — their title, seniority, sector, cultural background, career stage — also signal who DivaTech is *not* for. This is not a brand aesthetics question. It is a stakeholder and equity question embedded in the ICP itself.

Specific concern: if the champion persona is defined too narrowly (e.g. senior CHRO in a large GCC bank), the messaging may quietly exclude mid-market organisations, non-banking sectors, or champions who don't match that profile — limiting addressable market and creating reputational risk if the exclusion becomes visible.

This should be reviewed as a distinct lens when Morgan's ICP research lands and Casey is tagged in. Casey to provide Red Hat analysis on who the current framing may be leaving out and what the stakeholder/perception consequences are.

Owner: Casey (analysis, when TKT-002 unblocks)
Dependency: TKT-002 (Morgan's ICP research)
TKT-010.creator: Jordan

TKT-011.title: Content Release Protocol — messaging matrix deployment governance
TKT-011.description:
Alex's risk review of the messaging matrix (TKT-007) identified six risk flags. Rather than editing the matrix with defensive qualifiers — which would hollow out the creative system — the proposed response is a companion Content Release Protocol document.

The protocol maps each flagged claim type to a specific release condition: what evidence or decision is required before that element can be used externally. This separates the strategic/creative layer (the matrix) from the deployment governance layer (the protocol).

Scope:
- Map all ⚠️-flagged elements in messaging matrix to release conditions
- Define what constitutes "validated" for each claim type (proof data, compliance sign-off, Boss decision, TKT-002 output, etc.)
- Specify which elements are cleared for internal use now vs. external use pending validation
- Address fork discipline: no fork-dependent content goes external until fork is resolved
- Short document — likely 1-2 pages plus a reference table

Dependencies:
- Alex's risk register (TKT-007 output) — feeds directly into this
- TKT-002 outputs — will populate several release conditions
- Boss GTM decision — will activate certain fork-dependent sections
- Compliance/legal review (for measurement methodology and GCC regulatory claims)

Proposed owner: River (drafts), Alex (reviews — it is built on their risk register)
TKT-011.creator: River

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text: Sprint planning note: River proposes a staged approach. Once TKT-016 clears (~2 hours), River drafts the full TKT-011 skeleton — structure complete, all flagged elements mapped to release condition types, internal-use-cleared sections written, fork-dependent sections explicitly named as pending. Alex then reviews structure. Ticket moves to partially built / awaiting real dependency inputs rather than sitting invisible in backlog. Sequencing: TKT-016 → TKT-011 skeleton → Alex review → finalise when TKT-002 outputs and Boss GTM decision land. Jordan has visibility. No sequencing change required.

comments.author: Alex
comments.text:
Review expectation confirmed and on radar. Sequencing understood: TKT-016 → River skeleton → Alex structural review → hold pending TKT-002 outputs and Boss GTM decision.

Pre-review notes for River's benefit when drafting the skeleton:

1. The risk register from TKT-007 identified five distinct risk categories — the protocol structure should map cleanly against all five (unsubstantiated proof points, ROI/measurement claims, GCC-specific claims, fork-dependent content, pressure language). If River organises the skeleton by claim type rather than risk category, I will need to reverse-map during review. Worth aligning on structure before River drafts to save a revision cycle.

2. The fork discipline section is the most critical part of this document. TKT-002's ICP-FINAL.md is now complete, but the Boss GTM decision remains outstanding. That means fork-dependent content (CHRO vs CDO path variants, website path activation) is still live. The skeleton must have explicit placeholder language for this — not just "pending" but specifying *which* decision resolves *which* fork. Vague placeholders create ambiguity during the final fill-in pass.

3. TKT-002 is marked completed, but Morgan flagged two live gaps that feed TKT-011 release conditions: (a) ME local registration/legal check (DIFC/ADGM/SAMA) — any GCC-facing claims in the matrix cannot go external until this clears; (b) the warm lead identity (TKT-008) is unresolved. The protocol should treat GCC regulatory claims as a distinct release condition tier from general proof point claims.

4. Measurement methodology claims (the learning measurement toolkit from TKT-002 curriculum work) carry specific liability exposure. These need their own release condition — not just "TKT-002 complete" (which it now is) but "validated against an actual delivery." The protocol should distinguish between claims backed by published methodology and claims backed by actual outcome data.

Ready to review when the skeleton lands. Jordan — confirm timing once sprint is locked.

TKT-012.title: Champion vindication experience — post-sale client journey brief
TKT-012.description:
Casey's GTM analysis identified a gap that brand work alone cannot close: the post-sale product experience must make the internal champion feel publicly, visibly vindicated for having recommended DivaTech — not just deliver training outcomes to learners.

This ticket scopes the client experience brief that addresses this. Specifically:

- Progress reporting designed to be shareable upward by the champion
- "Early win" moments engineered into the programme delivery timeline (e.g. a Week 6 checkpoint the champion can take to their budget-holder)
- Metrics framing that speaks to the champion's internal political credibility, not just learner completion rates
- Consideration of how DivaTech communicates with the champion vs. the learner cohort throughout delivery

This is NOT a brand deliverable. It is a client experience / delivery design brief.

DEPENDENCY: This work is downstream of the GTM model decision (champion-led vs. committee-led) which is pending Boss sign-off. The brief should be scoped against the champion-led model as the working assumption (per Casey's analysis and current team direction), but formal kick-off should follow Boss's GTM decision.

SOURCE TRAIL:
- Casey's analysis: projects/divatech/gtm-model-analysis-casey.md
- Morgan flagged post-signature risk: TKT-002 comments (×2)
- River named the gap precisely: message to Jordan [current session]
- TKT-004 / Doc 06 covers pre-sale champion toolkit — this ticket covers post-sale
TKT-012.creator: Jordan

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text:
Ticket created following River's message identifying the post-sale champion experience gap. Gap had been noted by Morgan in TKT-002 comments but not yet scoped as a discrete piece of work. River named it precisely enough to brief from.

Holding in backlog pending Boss's GTM model decision. This ticket, TKT-002, and TKT-010 all share the same upstream dependency. Will move to ready as a cluster once that decision is confirmed.

TKT-014.title: Legal & Operational Foundations — Alex risk analysis complete
TKT-014.description:
Alex (Black Hat) has completed a full risk-structured analysis of DivaTech's six legal and operational foundations:

1. Business entity structure — UK Ltd recommended; GCC jurisdiction decision required
2. Name registration — Companies House + trademark search required before any commercial use
3. Business banking — HSBC Business (primary) + Wise (secondary) recommended; 3–6 week lead time
4. Client contracts & trainer agreements — CSA and TSA must be solicitor-drafted; IR35 is highest-probability financial risk
5. GDPR & data protection — ICO registration is a legal obligation before handling any personal data
6. IP ownership — pre-formation IP assignment and contractor IP assignment are critical before trading

Eight documents filed in F:\workspace\personality\projects\divatech\tkt-007 including:
- 00_MASTER_SUMMARY.md (updated)
- 01–06: individual workstream analyses
- 07_launch_readiness_tracker.md (three-gate action tracker to first client engagement)
- 08_ir35_assessment_framework.md (CEST framework and record template for every trainer)

Minimum safe timeline to first client engagement: 6–7 weeks from Gate 1 actions.

BLOCKED on 11 Boss decisions — see Decision Register in master summary. Five are CRITICAL (incorporation, pre-formation IP, solicitor instruction x2, ICO registration). Escalated to Boss.

comments.author: River
comments.text: REVISED ANALYSIS (supersedes earlier comment): TKT-015 is a PREREQUISITE to TKT-018, not a sibling. Reference cards are not decoration — they are the cognitive scaffolding that holds the session together. Running the pilot with raw Word docs would be a false test of the experience. TKT-015 must be complete before TKT-018 can run. Effort ~0.5 days, no dependencies — can start immediately.

comments.author: River

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text: River has offered to self-assign TKT-016 given deep familiarity with the GCC pack. Flagged for Boss confirmation during sprint planning conversation. Estimated effort ~2 hours. Standalone — no delivery dependencies. Can run anytime, including as stretch work during pilot prep week.

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text: River flags one content fork requiring Boss steer before work begins: should the one-sheet LEAD with the nationalisation angle, or hold it as a secondary proof point? Everything else writes from existing GCC pack material. This question needs to be answered alongside sprint planning sign-off — add to Boss escalation agenda.

TKT-017.title: 30-minute Exec Taster variant — senior sponsor engagement tool
TKT-017.description: A 30-minute executive taster session variant is needed for C-suite and senior sponsor preview conversations. Currently only a 60-minute taster exists (curriculum doc 09). This shorter format is needed before first senior sponsor engagement. Estimated effort: half day. Should retain the core design principles of the 60-min version but be ruthlessly edited for an exec audience — likely M1 (what it is) + M3 (practical value) only, with strong post-taster capture mechanism.
TKT-017.creator: Jordan

comments.author: River
comments.text: Sequencing analysis: River identifies TKT-017 as the load-bearing dependency. Rationale — the Exec Taster is the trust-builder and pre-commitment device. Executives who haven't experienced the content are a much harder room to pitch. The Taster gives Boss something concrete to send to DivaTech before the pilot is even booked. River recommends building this first. WILDCARD: River proposes a two-act format — first 30 mins as Taster (execs as participants), then flip the room (execs become stakeholders watching their teams). One event, two audiences. Flagged to Boss for sequencing decision.

comments.author: River
comments.text: REVISED ANALYSIS (supersedes earlier comment): TKT-017 is the pre-sell mechanism, not the delivery mechanism. It unlocks the conversation that books the client room — parallel track to TKT-015, not sequential. Needs to exist before DivaTech returns to a senior sponsor, but is not a gate on delivery day. Can run concurrently with TKT-015 as both are ~half-day efforts with different skill/output requirements.

comments.author: Jordan
comments.text: Build time estimate from River: standard 30-min Exec Taster variant = approx. half a day. Two-act format (if Boss approves) = 1 to 1.5 days. Fast turnaround either way. Factor into sprint planning once Boss responds.

TKT-018.title: Internal pilot delivery — DivaTech team run-through before client deployment
TKT-018.description: Before taking the GenAI Foundations curriculum to a paying client, an internal pilot run is needed with the DivaTech team as participants. Purpose: validate timing, surface missing examples, test facilitator guides under live conditions, and build team confidence in delivery. River recommends this as a prerequisite for client deployment. Needs a date set, a facilitator assigned, and a structured debrief/feedback capture process.
TKT-018.creator: Jordan

comments.author: River
comments.text: Sequencing analysis: River positions TKT-018 as the hard blocker — the pilot IS the DivaTech engagement, not preparation for it. However, River argues TKT-017 (Exec Taster) should precede this, as executive buy-in needs to be secured first. TKT-015 (reference cards) should land just before or alongside the pilot. TKT-016 (GCC one-sheet) is post-pilot — a sales/expansion asset, not a delivery prerequisite.

comments.author: River
comments.text: REVISED ANALYSIS (supersedes earlier comment): TKT-018 is the keystone — it's the only item that validates everything else under live conditions. Beautiful reference cards and a polished taster can still hide timing issues, jarring transitions, examples that don't land. The pilot is the proof-of-concept moment. Two Boss decisions gate this entirely: (1) Who facilitates — internal team member or external voice? (2) What is the pilot date — this is the peg everything else hangs on. Once those two answers exist, TKT-015/016/017 can all be assigned and moving in the same sprint.
